raw.title: The growing push to grant legal rights to nature
raw.excerpt: From Ecuador to New Zealand and India, a growing number of countries are recognizing the legal rights of nature, though not without legal and political challenges.

llm_processed.summary: The article examines global efforts to grant legal rights to nature, citing Ecuador's 2008 constitutional protections and similar initiatives in Spain, New Zealand, and India.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Enables integration of legal frameworks with ecological restoration, supporting rights-based approaches to conservation.
